text.title: AI Overview Monitoring Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that make AIO / Perplexity / ChatGPT-Search monitoring an actionable feedback loop, not a dashboard staring contest.

rule.statement: Monitoring runs WEEKLY at minimum — on a fixed weekday so trends can be compared like-for-like. Bi-weekly or monthly is REJECTED for active topic clusters. Snapshots MUST land within ±24h of the scheduled run time.
rule.rationale: AIO citations rotate on a 1-3 week cadence as Google re-evaluates source quality. Monthly monitoring misses the recovery window. Weekly is the floor that lets the team see drift while there is still time to act.

rule.statement: Every monitored query MUST be checked against ALL THREE citation sources: Google AIO, Perplexity, ChatGPT-Search. Monitoring only Google misses the 30-40% of share that Perplexity + ChatGPT carry in tech / SaaS niches as of 2026.
rule.rationale: Search has fragmented across three engines with different selection biases. AIO favours definitions and tables; Perplexity favours citation density; ChatGPT-Search favours recent + authoritative. A piece may be cited by one and not the others; single-source monitoring blinds the marketer.

rule.statement: Weekly snapshots are IMMUTABLE once written. Re-running the scrape and overwriting a snapshot is FORBIDDEN. If a scrape fails the snapshot includes the failure as a `partial` status; do not re-run silently to "fix" a bad reading.
rule.rationale: Mutable snapshots destroy the trend signal. The team's weekly review must be against the data that was actually true at scrape time, not against a re-scraped clean version. Failure modes are part of the signal.

rule.statement: Drops in citation MUST be mapped to an action via the documented ladder (`templates/action-ladder.md`). Permitted actions: rewrite_section, add_citations, refresh_publish_date, swap_table_format, deprioritize_query, abandon_query. Ad-hoc actions outside the ladder are RECORDED but ladder must be updated to formalise new actions before adoption becomes routine.
rule.rationale: Untyped actions produce one-off fixes that do not compound. The ladder is the institutional memory of what worked at what signal. Each action carries a measurable expected outcome to validate against the next snapshot.

rule.statement: The trend report MUST be reviewed by the responsible marketer weekly. Auto-actions from `action-router.py` are SUGGESTIONS; the marketer accepts, modifies, or declines. Auto-execution of rewrites or republish actions is FORBIDDEN.
rule.rationale: Marketing decisions affect brand voice; auto-rewriting content from scraper signal creates voice drift. The marketer is the gate; the pipeline produces the dashboard and the suggestion, not the change.

section.title: The action ladder (summary)

list.item: L1 add_citations: triggered when AIO drops AND content has < 3 primary citations in the relevant section.
L2 rewrite_section: triggered when AIO drops AND ChatGPT-Search no longer cites either.
L3 swap_table_format: triggered when Perplexity drops AND piece uses text-format "table" instead of ``.
L4 refresh_publish_date: triggered when freshness signal moves below niche threshold AND content is >= 12 months old.
L5 deprioritize_query: triggered after 3 weeks of failed remediation; query moves to watch list.
L6 abandon_query: triggered when query has zero citation across all three sources for 6+ weeks; redirect effort.

section.reference: Detailed ladder with triggers, expected outcomes, validation criteria.

example.title: L1 action triggered + validated week-over-week
example.code: Week 23: query "what is JTBD" — AIO=not_cited (was cited W18-W22), Perplexity=cited, ChatGPT=cited. Action: L1 add_citations to the JTBD pillar's "definition" section. Validation: re-scrape W24. AIO=cited. Action validated; logged outcome=success.
example.note: r4 + r5 satisfied. Action taken from ladder; weekly cadence validates within one cycle.

example.title: Silent re-scrape after failure
example.code: Week 23 scrape: Perplexity adapter returned 502s for half the queries. Action taken: re-ran scrape next day; overwrote W23 snapshot. Outcome: trend report missing the partial-failure data.
example.note: r3 violation. The partial failure is now invisible; future scraper drift won't be detected until it's worse.
